generated_comment: Arre yaar, every other word spoken in anger does not need a gender lecture attached to it. We grew up hearing these expressions in college, cricket, traffic, everywhere; nobody is literally talking about anyone's mother or sister. And frankly, if a man keeps whining that such words hurt his feelings, then he should toughen up instead of behaving in a soft, overly delicate way.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-degrading abuse as harmless expression and dismisses its gendered structure. It also encodes feminizing weakness as an insult by implying a man is lesser if he is 'soft' or emotionally affected.
original_frame: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where a young woman has written about her decision not to wear sindoor because she does not believe in the tradition and sees it as a patriarchal symbol. The post has divided the comment section. You are writing a detailed comment defending sindoor and what it represents and why you think women who reject these symbols are rejecting something more than just a cosmetic tradition and what you think their real motivations are.
